search_evidence: Front torsion OD was searched from low-speed push, body-slip stability, and BMW/Sebring front-platform safety evidence.
Rear spring rate was searched against exit rotation demand, traction risk, and rear tyre support evidence.
Rear ARB size was searched as part of the BMW/Sebring rotation-control stage.
Rear ARB blade was searched with long-exit corner weighting instead of staying at the baseline blade.
Front camber was searched using loaded-front support, exit push, and tyre thermal evidence.
Rear camber was searched from traction demand, body-slip stability, and rear tyre support.
Front toe was searched from low-speed push, yaw correlation, and front thermal support.
Rear toe was searched for the traction and rotation compromise instead of staying stability-only.
Preload was searched against exit-push, instability, and rear power-slip evidence.
The legal coupled ramp option was searched with extra weight on Sebring long-exit understeer.
Clutch plate count was searched both toward less lock and more lock based on exit and traction evidence.

solver_notes: Materialized candidate through shared solve chain.
Applied family-specific supporting overrides on top of fresh supporting solve.
BMW/Sebring rotation search scored 5670 abstract candidates.
Telemetry rotation state: entry_push=0.05; exit_push=3.22; instability=0.06; traction_risk=0.13; long_exit_bias=4.48.
Base rotation score 20.018: pred_exit=3.22; pred_entry=0.05; pred_instability=0.06; pred_traction=0.13.

Selected rotation profile 'free_exit_rotation' at 14.070: diff 20Nm / 50/75 / 6 plates; springs FTB 14.34 / R 130; toe F-0.6 R+0.2; camber F-2.2 R-1.6; RARB Medium/5 | pred_exit=1.05; pred_entry=0.00; pred_instability=0.82; pred_traction=0.84
Materialized 12 BMW/Sebring rotation candidates and selected the lowest-penalty combination.
Applied rematerialized incremental candidate result to final report/JSON/export payloads.
Preserved BMW/Sebring second-stage rotation controls after candidate-family rematerialization.

constraints.name: Torsion bar OD valid (14.34mm)
constraints.detail: OD 14.34mm not in discrete garage options

front_torsion_od_mm: Front torsion OD was searched from low-speed push, body-slip stability, and BMW/Sebring front-platform safety evidence.
rear_spring_rate_nmm: Rear spring rate was searched against exit rotation demand, traction risk, and rear tyre support evidence.

constraints.name: RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: Explicit ARB materialization preserves the selected bar/blade family and recomputes LLTD.
Front ARB Soft/1, rear ARB Medium/5.

rear_arb_size: Rear ARB size was searched as part of the BMW/Sebring rotation-control stage.
rear_arb_blade: Rear ARB blade was searched with long-exit corner weighting instead of staying at the baseline blade.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

notes: Explicit geometry materialization at representative roll 2.2°.

front_toe_mm: Front toe was searched from low-speed push, yaw correlation, and front thermal support.
rear_toe_mm: Rear toe was searched for the traction and rotation compromise instead of staying stability-only.
front_camber_deg: Front camber was searched using loaded-front support, exit push, and tyre thermal evidence.
rear_camber_deg: Rear camber was searched from traction demand, body-slip stability, and rear tyre support.

step6_dampers.hs_slope_reasoning: Front p99/p95=2.06 -> slope 11, Rear p99/p95=2.02 -> slope 11

constraints.name: Front LS damping ratio
constraints.note: 0.3-1.0 valid range for racing. GTP front uses high ζ due to soft springs.

constraints.name: Rear HS damping ratio
constraints.note: Must be low for rear traction over bumps. >0.4 = snap oversteer risk.

constraints.name: Front HS rbd > HS comp
constraints.note: Rebound must exceed comp to prevent wheel bounce.

constraints.name: Rear HS comp < Front HS comp
constraints.note: Compliance hierarchy: rear yields to bumps more than front.

constraints.name: Front LS comp >= Rear LS comp
constraints.note: Front controls entry. If violated: car will be nervous on entry.

constraints.name: Rear shock oscillation vs natural freq
constraints.note: Ratio: 2.65x natural freq (3.30 Hz). >1.5x = underdamped evidence. ζ_hs_rear bumped to 0.250.

notes: Front critical damping: 9552 N*s/m (modal k=82.6 N/mm, f_n = 2.75 Hz)
Rear critical damping: 12787 N*s/m (modal k=132.7 N/mm, f_n = 3.30 Hz)
Front LS: zeta=0.66 -> c=6274 N*s/m -> F@25mm/s = 157 N -> 9 clicks
Front HS: zeta=0.22 -> c=2122 N*s/m -> F@129mm/s = 274 N -> 3 clicks
Rear HS: zeta=0.25 -> c=3197 N*s/m -> F@169mm/s = 542 N -> 7 clicks
HS ref velocities: front p95=128.9mm/s, rear p95=169.4mm/s (rear +31% more active)
Damping ratios are derived from quarter-car eigenvalue analysis, NOT from empirical baseline matching.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 46.0% | Fuel correction: -0.02% at 40L | Result: 46.0%; +0.7% from trail brake depth p95=0.79; +0.2% for high braking decel p95=2.33g; -0.5% for front braking lock proxy p95=0.083; ⚠ High brake deceleration asymmetry (23.3 m/s²) detected — check front caliper/pad condition before adjusting brake bias; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=23.3 m/s^2; Note: measured hydraulic split 71.8% vs recommended 46.2% (delta +25.6%); Current target -1.0 retained as hardware context only; MC: F/R = 20.6/22.2 mm (ratio 0.93); differs from nominal 1.00 — effective bias may need decrease; Pad: Medium — low brake temps, softer compound may improve initial bite | front-lock evidence shifted brake hardware and migration rearward
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, master cylinders, pad compound, and migration were conservatively seeded from braking evidence.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 28.9 Nm (car default 12 Nm; lat transfer=14468N at peak lat_g=4.24g); +5 Nm for rear power slip p95=0.080; clutch plates=6 | Coast 45 deg (from trail brake depth=0.21); Drive 70 deg (from throttle R^2=0.61) | Lock: coast=62.2% drive=27.2% (preload 7.1% + plates 55.1%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; +1 gain for erratic consistency; +1 slip for rear power slip p95=0.080; +1 gain: TC barely active (0%) but rear slip p95=0.080; +1 slip for high MGU-K torque 348 Nm; anchored to driver-loaded gain=4; anchored to driver-loaded slip=4
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 23°C → cold adj +2.1 kPa; Front hot 181 kPa → cold 152 kPa; Rear hot 176 kPa → cold 152 kPa

diff_preload_nm: Preload was searched against exit-push, instability, and rear power-slip evidence.
diff_ramp_option_idx: The legal coupled ramp option was searched with extra weight on Sebring long-exit understeer.
diff_clutch_plates: Clutch plate count was searched both toward less lock and more lock based on exit and traction evidence.

warnings: ARB has not been auto-validated. To upgrade to high confidence, run auto_calibrate with 3+ IBT sessions varying ARB sizes.
